override_reason: Sponsoring consumer release lead unreachable within the 7-business-day window per parity-protocol.md §8 timeout policy. No designated alternate available. Maintainer commits the handoff alone under the deferred-co-sign window; consumer co-signs retroactively when available. Failure to co-sign within 30 days post-tag triggers a parity-protocol PATCH bump (1.0.0 -> 1.0.1) recording the asymmetric ratification.
warn_window_basis: (PR-A1.6 merged + 48h) OR signed v8.4.0 tag fire — whichever first; per parity-protocol.md section 6. CI gates on this exact ISO-8601 timestamp; no recomputation occurs after the handoff is committed.
deferred_co_sign_note: 60 days from co_signed_at. Consumer co-signature accepted any time before this date. Past this date, the asymmetric ratification is recorded via a parity-protocol PATCH bump (1.0.0 -> 1.0.1) per parity-protocol.md section 8. The 60-day window absorbs typical holiday + on-call rotation gaps that a 30-day window does not.
